title: PTE Score Calculator - Overall Score from Section Scores
tagline: Convert your PTE Academic section scores into an overall score instantly. Understand your English proficiency level for university and visa applications.
intro: Enter your Listening, Reading, Speaking, and Writing scores (10–90) to calculate your overall PTE Academic score.

error.invalid: Please enter valid scores between 10 and 90 for all four sections.
error.range: Each score must be a whole number between 10 and 90.

about.heading: About the PTE Score Calculator
about.body:
The Pearson Test of English (PTE) Academic is a computer-based English language exam used by universities, professional licensing bodies, and immigration authorities worldwide. Unlike some competing tests, PTE Academic is entirely machine-scored — no human examiner ever grades a response — which makes the scoring process fast, consistent, and highly reproducible across test centres and dates.

PTE Academic assesses four core language skills: Listening, Reading, Speaking, and Writing. Each section is graded on a scale from 10 to 90, where 90 represents an expert-level command of English and 10 represents the most basic functional ability. The overall score is the simple arithmetic mean of the four section scores, rounded to the nearest whole number. For example, section scores of 65, 70, 65, and 70 average to 67.5, which rounds to 68.

The 10–90 scale was designed to provide granular differentiation across the full proficiency spectrum. Unlike percentage-based systems, the PTE scale avoids the psychological distortion of scores near 0 or 100 and provides equal spacing across each proficiency band. Institutions can therefore set precise cut-offs that reflect their actual language-readiness requirements rather than rounding to convenient multiples.

Understanding what each score range means in practice is essential for setting realistic preparation targets. Scores of 65 and above are generally considered 'Good' and meet the requirements of most postgraduate programmes in English-speaking countries. Scores of 79 and above satisfy the requirements of many medical and nursing registration bodies in Australia and the UK. Immigration points-based systems, such as Australia's General Skilled Migration pathway, typically award maximum points at a score of 65 or above and require a minimum score in each individual section to prevent candidates from compensating a weak skill with a strong one.

Speaking and Writing are assessed by Pearson's Automated Scoring Engine (ASE), which evaluates oral fluency, pronunciation, content accuracy, grammar, vocabulary range, and written discourse structure. This AI-based scoring removes rater fatigue and inter-rater variability while producing scores that correlate strongly with human expert judgment in validation studies.

For test-takers aiming to improve, the section breakdown provided by this calculator is valuable for targeted preparation. A candidate with a low Speaking score should focus on oral fluency and pronunciation drills, while a candidate with a low Reading score may benefit from timed skimming and scanning practice. Because the overall score is a simple average, improving the weakest section produces the greatest gain for a given amount of study time.

This calculator accepts whole numbers from 10 to 90 for each section, mirrors the arithmetic the official score report performs, and also maps the overall score to the corresponding proficiency descriptor so you can immediately understand how institutions and immigration authorities will interpret your result.

examples.heading: PTE score calculation examples
examples.intro: Four scenarios illustrating how section scores combine into an overall PTE Academic score.

items.note: Strong academic performance. Meets most graduate programme requirements and satisfies many professional registration bodies.
Balanced performance suitable for undergraduate admission at most English-medium universities.
Minimum competence level. Meets minimum entry requirements for some undergraduate programmes.
Excellent performance. Satisfies the highest-tier immigration and professional registration requirements.

howto.heading: How to use the PTE score calculator
steps: Locate your official PTE Academic Score Report and find the four individual section scores for Listening, Reading, Speaking, and Writing.
Enter each section score in the corresponding field. Scores must be whole numbers between 10 and 90.
Click Calculate Score. The calculator displays your overall score as the rounded average of all four sections, plus the corresponding proficiency level.
Review the section breakdown to identify your weakest area — improving the lowest section produces the greatest gain in your overall score.
Click Reset to clear all fields and start again with different scores.

items.q: How is the overall PTE score calculated?
items.a: The overall PTE Academic score is the simple arithmetic mean of the four section scores (Listening, Reading, Speaking, Writing), rounded to the nearest whole number. If the average ends in exactly .5, it rounds up. This calculator replicates that exact calculation.

items.q: What is a good PTE score?
items.a: A score of 65 or above is generally considered 'Good' and meets the English language requirements of most undergraduate and postgraduate programmes at English-medium universities. Scores of 79+ satisfy the requirements of most medical and nursing registration bodies in Australia, New Zealand, and the UK. Immigration programmes typically require 50–65 depending on the visa category.

items.q: How long are PTE scores valid?
items.a: PTE Academic scores are valid for two years from the test date. After two years, scores cannot be submitted to institutions or immigration authorities and you would need to retake the test. Always verify the score validity requirements of your specific institution or visa application before booking a test.

items.q: Can I combine scores from different test dates?
items.a: No. PTE Academic does not allow score combination from different test dates. Your overall score and all four section scores must come from a single test sitting. Some institutions may consider your best result across multiple sittings, but each result is calculated independently from its own four section scores.

items.q: Why does each section have different minimum requirements for some visas?
items.a: Many immigration programmes set individual section minimums to prevent candidates from compensating a critical weakness with other strong skills. For example, a healthcare worker needs strong Listening and Speaking regardless of Writing ability. Always check both the overall score requirement and any per-section minimums for your specific visa or registration pathway.

items.q: What is the difference between PTE Academic and PTE Core?
items.a: PTE Academic is designed for higher education and skilled migration, tested at official test centres. PTE Core is a newer format aimed at Canadian immigration and economic migration pathways, with a slightly different task format and scoring methodology. This calculator is designed for PTE Academic scores. If you are taking PTE Core, consult the official Pearson guidance for score interpretation.
